title: The Mentor's Handbook
subtitle: Teaching Harness Engineering — everything a mentor must learn, from the agent loop up, to deliver the five-day workshop and build a pi-style harness live with the cohort.
for: Dr. Raj Dandekar & the Vizuara teaching team

parts.title: Before You Teach
parts.blurb: The mindset and the single story that connects all five days: build a coding harness like pi, one layer at a time.

chapters.title: How to use this handbook
chapters.blurb: The seven ingredients in every chapter, and how to prep a live day from it: learn the layer, then teach it while building it live.

chapters.title: The whole arc: five layers, one harness
chapters.blurb: The story that ties the week together: loop, tools, context, durability, orchestration, assembled into a working pi-style harness by Friday. Drawn as one map.

parts.title: Foundations You Must Feel
parts.blurb: The bedrock: what a harness even is, the three engineering disciplines, and the five layers, explained so simply a beginner keeps up.

chapters.title: Why an agent needs a harness
chapters.blurb: The model is a brain in a jar; the harness is the body. Taught with a metaphor a student never forgets, then tied to Claude Code and pi.

chapters.title: Prompt vs. context vs. harness, simply
chapters.blurb: Three disciplines drawn as three concentric circles: what you say, what the model sees, what it lives inside. The boundary students always blur, and the fix.

chapters.title: The five layers, drawn
chapters.blurb: Loop, tools, context, durability, orchestration as a stack you build up. The map you draw on the board on day one and return to all week.

parts.title: Teaching the Loop
parts.blurb: Day 1. How to teach the agent loop from first principles and get the class to a working bare harness.

chapters.title: Teaching the agent loop
chapters.blurb: The whole of agency as a while-loop, taught with a to-do-list metaphor. The board sequence, the live demo, and the aha moment.

chapters.title: Teaching messages, turns & state
chapters.blurb: The message array as the agent's whole memory-so-far. How to make 'the array is the state' click, and where students get confused.

chapters.title: Teaching the first bare harness (live build)
chapters.blurb: How to build the smallest real agent live: model client, message array, loop. Timings, what to type, what to let them try.

parts.title: Teaching Tools & Safety
parts.blurb: Day 2. How to teach tool schemas, wiring real file/shell tools into the loop, and the permission + sandbox story.

chapters.title: Teaching tools as contracts
chapters.blurb: A tool schema as a job description the model reads. The metaphor, a by-hand example, and how to teach why the schema shape matters.

chapters.title: Teaching permissions & sandboxing
chapters.blurb: Why the agent asks before rm, and the blast-radius idea taught as a kitchen with a fire door. The safety story every student should internalize.

chapters.title: Teaching the tools build (live)
chapters.blurb: Wiring read/write/edit/bash into the loop so the harness edits real code safely, live. The demo that makes students gasp.

parts.title: Teaching Context Engineering
parts.blurb: Day 3. How to teach the context window as a scarce resource, compaction, and memory.

chapters.title: Teaching compaction & memory
chapters.blurb: The context window as a small desk you keep tidying, with a notebook (CLAUDE.md) for what must survive. Metaphors, the running-summary trick, and the demo.

chapters.title: Teaching the context engine (live build)
chapters.blurb: Building compaction + persistent memory into the loop so the harness survives a long session. How to pace it and what to profile with the class.

parts.title: Teaching Durability & Orchestration
parts.blurb: Day 4. How to teach checkpointing, recovery, and sub-agents under supervision.

chapters.title: Teaching checkpointing & recovery
chapters.blurb: Durable execution as a video-game save point, taught simply. Why replay returns cached results, and the crash-and-resume demo that lands it.

chapters.title: Teaching sub-agents & orchestration
chapters.blurb: When one context can't hold the job: spawn a focused helper, get its answer back. The delegation metaphor, plan-mode, and the human-in-the-loop gate.

parts.title: Delivery Craft
parts.blurb: Turning understanding into five great live days, and running the capstone.

chapters.title: Lecture plans: Days 1-3, block by block
chapters.blurb: Timings, board sequences, the live-build checkpoints, and the questions to ask for the loop, tools, and context days.

chapters.title: Lecture plans: Days 4-5, block by block
chapters.blurb: Durability + orchestration, then the production-harness dissections (pi, Hermes, Claude Code) and the capstone kickoff.

chapters.title: Running the capstone: their own harness
chapters.blurb: How to set up, mentor, and review each student assembling their own pi-style harness, and how to run the final demos.
